title: Parkio Daily for June 8, 2026
teaser: La Poutinerie opens July 1 at EPCOT; Mickey's Not-So-Scary showtimes confirmed; Magic Kingdom moat refilled after castle makeover.

rides.name: Space Mountain
rides.note: Castle work finished; Tomorrowland crowds lighter post-construction

rides.name: Test Track
rides.note: World Showcase morning lull before La Poutinerie buzz starts

rides.name: Flight of Passage
rides.note: Bluey's Wild World drawing families later in the day

breaking.title: La Poutinerie opens July 1 at EPCOT with full menu
breaking.body: The former Refreshment Port will reopen as La Poutinerie on Canada Day, serving poutine variations including classic, pulled pork, and vegetarian options. Air Canada sponsors the updated quick-service spot.

parkioInsight.text: Opening day crowds for new food venues tend to peak around lunch. Stopping by mid-afternoon on July 1 may offer shorter waits and fresher batches.

breaking.title: Mickey's Not-So-Scary showtimes confirmed for fall parties
breaking.body: Disney confirmed the Not-So-Spooky Spectacular fireworks will launch at 10pm during this year's Halloween party nights at Magic Kingdom. Dessert party reservations are now open.

parkioInsight.text: Hub viewing areas for the 10pm show usually fill by 9:30pm on party nights. Claiming a spot near the castle right after the first parade often secures better sightlines.

bignews.title: Magic Kingdom moat refilled, completing castle makeover
bignews.body: Crews have refilled the moat around Cinderella Castle, marking the unofficial end of the central plaza renovation. The updated look includes refreshed landscaping and improved guest flow.

parkioInsight.text: The newly finished hub offers unobstructed photo angles from multiple sides. Early morning light before 9am typically provides the clearest shots without large crowds.

bignews.title: Disney Princess Half Marathon registration opens today after delay
bignews.body: Registration for the 2027 Disney Princess Half Marathon Weekend goes live today following a postponement. The runDisney event at Walt Disney World includes multiple race distances.

parkioInsight.text: Race weekends usually bring heavier morning crowds to Magic Kingdom and EPCOT. Planning park time after 2pm on race days often avoids the bulk of runner traffic.

bignews.title: Animal Kingdom rhino undergoes successful cataract surgery
bignews.body: Veterinarians removed a cataract from Kendi, a 27-year-old white rhino who has lived at the park since birth. The procedure restored vision in her right eye after keepers noticed cloudiness.

parkioInsight.text: Kilimanjaro Safaris early departures often provide the best visibility for spotting rhinos near the watering holes. Booking a morning safari tends to increase wildlife activity.

topstories.title: Polynesian Resort construction reaches major milestone
topstories.body: The new arrival experience at Disney's Polynesian Resort hit a key phase over the weekend, with rerouted bus and vehicle entrances taking shape. Construction impacts remain ongoing around the property.

parkioInsight.text: Resort construction noise and detours can affect morning routines. Guests staying at the Polynesian may want to allow extra travel time to bus stops through mid-summer.

topstories.title: Toy Story Little Words Project collection launches ahead of film
topstories.body: A new bracelet and bag charm collection inspired by Toy Story characters is now available, timed to the June 19 release of Toy Story 5. Prices start at $30.

parkioInsight.text: Limited-edition collections tied to movie releases often sell out within the first week. Checking flagship merchandise locations like Emporium before noon may improve availability.

topstories.title: Muppets apparel arrives at Disney California Adventure
topstories.body: A cream-colored hoodie and matching t-shirt featuring the Muppets cast in colorful squares are now on shelves at Disneyland Resort. The adult hoodie retails for $64.99.

parkioInsight.text: Character apparel tied to legacy franchises tends to move quickly at smaller Disneyland shops. Visiting Elias & Co. or Off the Page early in the day may secure your size.

topstories.title: Club Cool removes two international soda flavors at EPCOT
topstories.body: Two beverage options have disappeared from the free tasting station at Club Cool in recent days. The removal affects international Coca-Cola flavors previously available to guests.

parkioInsight.text: Club Cool lineups usually spike mid-afternoon as guests seek air conditioning. Stopping by during the morning or evening hours tends to offer quicker access to the remaining flavors.

topstories.title: Disneyland announces 2026 Halloween season details
topstories.body: Fall festivities at Disneyland Resort will include ride overlays, themed merchandise, and pumpkin spice treats. The Disney Parks Blog shared initial planning information for the seasonal celebration.

parkioInsight.text: Halloween overlays like Haunted Mansion Holiday typically see the longest waits in the first two weeks. Visiting in late October often means shorter lines with the same experience.

icymi.title: Monstropolis construction progress visible at Hollywood Studios
icymi.body: Steel framework for the Monsters, Inc. land continues to rise behind construction walls at Hollywood Studios. Disney shared a construction update highlighting the emerging structures.

icymi.title: Cool Kids' Summer snacks under $10 available at Walt Disney World
icymi.body: Limited-time treats priced under $10 are part of the Cool Kids' Summer promotion across the resort. Options include both sweet and savory items tied to the seasonal event.

icymi.title: Disney Dining Plans add new customization for 2027 bookings
icymi.body: Starting with 2027 vacations, guests can personalize their dining plan selections with the new Deluxe tier. The Disney Parks Blog detailed the updated structure and options.

spotlight.title: Tomorrowland's quiet transformation nears completion at Magic Kingdom
spotlight.body: Imagineering has spent years subtly updating Tomorrowland's aesthetic, moving away from dated retro-futurism toward a refreshed look. The changes include new paint schemes, updated signage, and cohesive design language that better aligns with current attraction offerings.

parkioInsight.text: The updated land feels most impressive during evening hours when new lighting accents the design. Visiting Tomorrowland after 7pm often coincides with cooler temperatures and shorter attraction waits.
